# Copilot Instructions for app-orders-api

## Repository Purpose

This is the **orders-api** application repository in the IDP (Internal Developer Platform).
It contains application source code, Kubernetes manifests, and an **intent contract** (`infra/deploy.yaml`).

## Intent Contract (`infra/deploy.yaml`)

The `deploy.yaml` file is the **single interface** between developers and the platform.
Developers describe WHAT they need; the platform resolves HOW to provision it.

**When a developer asks to add resources, change scaling, update SLOs, or modify infrastructure — update `deploy.yaml`, NEVER write Terraform, Terragrunt, or Kustomize directly.**

### Schema Reference (ApplicationIntent v1)

```yaml
apiVersion: idp.platform/v1      # Always this value
kind: ApplicationIntent           # Always this value

metadata:
  name: <string>                  # App name (lowercase, hyphens, 3-63 chars)
  ownerTeam: <string>             # Team name (e.g., "commerce")
  costCenter: <string>            # Cost center code (e.g., "CC1234")

spec:
  environments:                   # Which environments to deploy to
    - dev                         # Valid: dev, uat, prod
    - uat
    - prod

  compute:
    platform: aks                 # Only "aks" supported
    workloadType: web-api         # web-api | worker | cron
    containerImage: <registry/image>
    autoscaling:
      minReplicas: 2              # Minimum: 1, default: 2
      maxReplicas: 10             # Minimum: 1, default: 10

  exposure:
    ingress: private              # private | public
    dnsZone: <string>             # Optional DNS zone

  data:
    postgres:
      enabled: true               # true | false
      tier: general-purpose-small  # See tiers below
    redis:
      enabled: false              # true | false (placeholder — module not yet built)

  security:
    complianceTier: standard      # standard | high
    secrets: keyvault             # Only "keyvault" supported

  reliability:
    slo: "99.9"                   # Format: "99", "99.9", "99.95"
```

### PostgreSQL Tiers (Resolution Table)

| Tier | Azure SKU | Storage | HA | Backup |
|------|-----------|---------|-----|--------|
| `burstable-small` | B_Standard_B1ms | 32 GB | Disabled | 7 days |
| `general-purpose-small` | GP_Standard_D2ds_v5 | 32 GB | Disabled | 14 days |
| `general-purpose-medium` | GP_Standard_D4ds_v5 | 64 GB | SameZone | 21 days |
| `memory-optimized-large` | MO_Standard_E4ds_v5 | 128 GB | SameZone | 35 days |

### Compute Workload Types

| Type | Service | HPA | NetworkPolicy | Default Port |
|------|---------|-----|---------------|:------------:|
| `web-api` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | 8080 |
| `worker` | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | — |
| `cron` | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | — |

## How It Works

1. Developer edits `deploy.yaml` (or Copilot does it via issue)
2. Push triggers the Intent Resolver workflow (`on-intent-change.yml`)
3. Resolver validates schema → generates Terragrunt configs + K8s overlays
4. Cross-repo PRs created: one in `platform-infra-live` (Azure), one here (K8s)
5. Review + merge → Terragrunt apply (Azure resources) + ArgoCD sync (K8s)

## Rules for Modifying deploy.yaml

- ONLY modify `infra/deploy.yaml` — never create Terraform, Terragrunt, or Kustomize files
- Validate against the schema above — all fields must use valid enum values
- Keep `apiVersion: idp.platform/v1` and `kind: ApplicationIntent` unchanged
- `metadata.name` must match the app name (`orders-api`)
- When adding a database, set `data.postgres.enabled: true` and choose a tier
- When scaling, adjust `compute.autoscaling.minReplicas` and `maxReplicas`
- When promoting to production, add `prod` to `spec.environments`
- Add a brief comment if the change is non-obvious

## Common Change Examples

### Add PostgreSQL database
```yaml
data:
  postgres:
    enabled: true
    tier: burstable-small    # Dev-appropriate tier
```

### Scale up for production
```yaml
compute:
  autoscaling:
    minReplicas: 3
    maxReplicas: 20
```

### Promote to UAT
```yaml
environments:
  - dev
  - uat
```

### Increase SLO target
```yaml
reliability:
  slo: "99.95"
```

### Switch to high compliance
```yaml
security:
  complianceTier: high
```

## Repository Structure

```
app-orders-api/
├── src/                    # Application source code
├── infra/
│   ├── deploy.yaml         # ★ Intent contract — the ONLY file developers edit
│   ├── base/               # K8s base manifests (generated by resolver)
│   └── overlays/           # K8s per-environment overlays (generated by resolver)
├── Dockerfile
└── .github/workflows/
    ├── ci.yml              # Build + test
    ├── build-push.yml      # Build + push to ACR
    └── on-intent-change.yml # Triggers intent resolver on deploy.yaml changes
```
